paragraph_content.content:  A modern SSD is a complicated system that consists of multiple cores, HW controllers, DRAM, and NAND flash memory packages
 Communication with the host operating system (receives & returns requests)
Via a certain interface (SATA or NVMe)
 Buffering data to write (read from NAND flash memory)

item_content.content: Helpful for improving lifetime (not so likely)
 Needs to ensure data integrity even under sudden power-off
Most DRAM capacity is used for L2P mappings

paragraph_content.content:  4 Bytes for 4 KiB  0.1% of SSD capacity

item_content.content: Selects a victim block  copies all valid pages  erase the victim block
 Evenly distributes P/E cycles across NAND flash blocks

paragraph_content.content:  Refresh pages with long retention ages

item_content.content: To avoid worst-case data patterns that can lead to significant errors
Can detect/correct errors: e.g., 72 bits/1 KiB error-correction capability
Stores additional parity information together with raw data

paragraph_content.content:  First checks if the request data exists in the write buffer
 If so, returns the corresponding request immediately with the data
 A host read request can be involved with several pages
Such a request can be returned only after all the requested data is ready
 Finds the PPA where the request data is stored from the L2P mapping table

item_content.content:  First reads the raw data from the flash chip

paragraph_content.content: Retries reading of the page w/ adjusted

paragraph_content.content: a special material: Floating gate (2D) or Charge trap (3D)

item_content.content:  w/ a special material: Floating gate (2D) or Charge trap (3D)
Can hold electrons in a non-volatile manner

paragraph_content.content: Multi-leveling: A flash cell can store multiple bits
Retention loss: A cell leaks electrons over time

paragraph_content.content: Limited lifetime: A cell wears out after P/E cycling
 Multiple (e.g., 128) flash cells are serially connected

Eraseing a cell  Decreasing the cell’s

paragraph_content.content:  Programming a page cannot change ‘0’ cells to ‘1’ cells  Erase-before-write property

item_content.content: Makes in-place write on a page very inefficient  Out-of-place write & GC

paragraph_content.content:  A large number (> 1,000) of blocks share bitlines in a plane
